title: color-scheme
description: Tells the browser which color scheme an element supports. Setting color-scheme: dark causes the browser to render native controls — inputs, checkboxes, scrollbars — in dark mode, even without custom styles on each one. Baseline newly available since 2022.
html:
Without color-scheme: dark

With color-scheme: dark
challenge: The left form has white native controls that look wrong on the dark background. Add color-scheme: dark to .form-wrapper.dark so the browser renders all its native controls in dark mode automatically.
realWorldUsage: Apply color-scheme: dark to the root element of a dark-mode layout instead of manually styling every input, select, scrollbar, and native control. It also improves the rendering of system UI components like date pickers and color pickers in dark contexts.
